abstract: Engineering rules for AI agents maintaining Containerlab, structured as 27 granular rules across 7 categories prioritized by impact: user compatibility, operational lifecycle safety, architecture/extension boundaries, link/endpoint/node/runtime contracts, topology/schema/docs alignment, Go context/error/logging practices, and testing strategy. Each rule is a single focused do/don't with an incorrect and correct example grounded in the real codebase. The core architecture rule — delegate to the owning interface and register new types rather than dispatching on a link type, node kind, or runtime name — spans every subsystem, the largest being the 60+ node kinds.
